text: The conditional proposition \(p \rightarrow q\) is false only when _____.
Which statement correctly represents the contrapositive of \(p \rightarrow q\)?
How is the statement "You will mow Mr. Smith's lawn only if he pays you" represented logically?
What is true about the biconditional statement \(p \leftrightarrow q\)?
answer: It is true when \(p\) and \(q\) have the same truth value
a: It is true if \(p\) is true and \(q\) is false
b: It is false when \(p\) and \(q\) have the same truth value
c: It is true when \(p\) and \(q\) have the same truth value
d: It is false if both \(p\) and \(q\) are true

text: The converse of the statement “If it rains, then the game is cancelled” is _____.
answer: If the game is cancelled, then it rains

c: If the game is not cancelled, then it does not rain
d: If it does not rain, then the game is not cancelled
